seo_description: Current time in Maputo, Mozambique. Live CAT clock — UTC+2 year-round, no Daylight Saving Time. Maputo time vs London, Johannesburg, Dubai, Nairobi.

faq.question: What time is it in Maputo right now?
faq.answer: Maputo operates on Central Africa Time (CAT), which is UTC+2 year-round. Mozambique does not observe Daylight Saving Time, so the offset never changes. Use the live clock above to see the exact current time in Maputo with live seconds.

faq.question: What time zone is Maputo in?
faq.answer: Maputo is in the Central Africa Time zone (CAT), fixed at UTC+2 throughout the entire year. This places Maputo 2 hours ahead of Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) and in the same timezone as Johannesburg (South Africa), Harare (Zimbabwe), Lusaka (Zambia), Gaborone (Botswana), and Windhoek (Namibia). Maputo is 2 hours behind Dubai and 7 hours ahead of New York during Eastern Standard Time.

faq.question: Does Maputo observe Daylight Saving Time?
faq.answer: No. Mozambique does not observe Daylight Saving Time. Maputo remains on CAT (UTC+2) throughout the entire year. This consistent offset makes scheduling with Maputo predictable, though the gap with DST-observing cities like London and New York fluctuates seasonally when those cities adjust their clocks.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Maputo and London?
faq.answer: Maputo is 2 hours ahead of London during winter (GMT/UTC+0) and 1 hour ahead during summer (BST/UTC+1). When it is 12:00 PM in London in January, it is 2:00 PM in Maputo. In summer, when London switches to BST, the gap narrows to just 1 hour — 12:00 PM London corresponds to 1:00 PM in Maputo.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Maputo and Johannesburg?
faq.answer: There is no time difference between Maputo and Johannesburg. Both cities operate on Central Africa Time (CAT/UTC+2) year-round with no Daylight Saving Time. This makes cross-border business and travel between Mozambique and South Africa easy to schedule, and reflects the close economic ties between the two countries.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Maputo and Dubai?
faq.answer: Maputo is 2 hours behind Dubai year-round. Dubai operates on Gulf Standard Time (GST/UTC+4) with no DST, and Maputo is on CAT (UTC+2) with no DST, making this a constant 2-hour difference. When it is 2:00 PM in Dubai, it is 12:00 PM (noon) in Maputo.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Maputo from the US?
faq.answer: From New York (EST/UTC-5), Maputo is 7 hours ahead in winter and 6 hours ahead in summer (EDT). To reach Maputo during business hours (8:00 AM – 5:00 PM CAT), East Coast callers should dial between 1:00 AM – 10:00 AM Eastern in winter. In practice, an early-morning New York call (7:00–9:00 AM Eastern) corresponds to a comfortable mid-morning slot (2:00–4:00 PM) in Maputo.

faq.question: What are business hours in Maputo?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Maputo run 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM or 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM CAT (UTC+2), Monday through Friday. Government offices close at 3:30 PM or 4:00 PM. Portuguese is the official language of Mozambique, and the capital's business environment reflects its colonial heritage and modern port-city economy. Maputo is a growing hub for natural gas investment, port trade, and tourism targeting the Bazaruto Archipelago and Inhambane Province.

content_blocks.title: Maputo Time Zone — CAT UTC+2 Year-Round
content_blocks.content: Maputo, the capital and largest city of Mozambique, operates on Central Africa Time (CAT/UTC+2) year-round with no Daylight Saving Time. This places Maputo in the same timezone as Johannesburg, Harare, Lusaka, and Windhoek — the broad band of southern and central African capitals that share UTC+2 permanently. Maputo is 2 hours ahead of London in winter, 1 hour ahead when London is on BST in summer, 2 hours behind Dubai (a constant, since neither observes DST), and 7 hours ahead of New York in winter. The city sits on the southern tip of Mozambique facing Maputo Bay and the Indian Ocean, making it geographically close to South Africa's border. Its stable UTC+2 timezone reflects the southern African bloc's consistent timekeeping, making regional business coordination straightforward.

content_blocks.title: Maputo — Indian Ocean Port City and Emerging Economy
content_blocks.content: Maputo is Mozambique's economic and cultural capital, perched on Maputo Bay along the Indian Ocean coast near the South African border. The city's port handles vast regional trade and is a major transit point for goods destined for landlocked neighbours including Zimbabwe, Zambia, and Malawi. Mozambique's economy has been transformed in recent years by major offshore natural gas discoveries in the Rovuma Basin — multinational energy companies including TotalEnergies, ENI, and ExxonMobil have major operations linked to Maputo. Tourism is growing steadily, driven by pristine beaches, the world-class Bazaruto Archipelago marine park, and Maputo's vibrant Portuguese-Mozambican cultural scene. The capital is home to the Maputo Stock Exchange (BVM) and a developing financial services sector, with Portuguese, Bantu languages, and English used in business contexts.

content_blocks.title: Calling and Working with Maputo Internationally
content_blocks.content: Maputo's CAT (UTC+2) offset aligns it closely with European business hours, making it a natural partner for companies in the UK, Portugal, France, and Germany. From London in winter (GMT), the 2-hour gap means a 9:00 AM London start corresponds to 11:00 AM in Maputo — excellent overlap. In summer (BST), the gap narrows to 1 hour. From Lisbon (WET/WEST — Portugal's official language ties to Mozambique are strong), the offset is 2 hours in winter and 1 hour in summer. From Dubai (UTC+4), Maputo is 2 hours behind — early Dubai afternoons align with Maputo mornings. From New York (EST), the 7-hour gap means morning New York calls correspond to Maputo's late afternoon — practicable, with some effort. From Tokyo (UTC+9), Maputo is 7 hours behind. Mozambique's country code is +258, and Maputo's city code is 21.
